title: The 2020 Election Timeline
summary: The record's most falsifiable moment — the October 2020 prediction, the 'temporal interference' caveat, and the course-correction doctrine that followed.
markdown:
**The 2020 Election** is where the record meets the checkable world most directly. In the
weeks before the vote, the sessions committed to a prediction — preserved here exactly as
spoken, in the archive's standing practice of keeping forecasts that did and did not
arrive.

The October 8, 2020 session states it flat: *"There is a battle in consciousness of your
collective. Donald J. Trump will be elected again as president... There will be a conflict
of interest between the two supportive groups that will cause a civil rivalry."* The
October 22 session repeats the call — *"Donald J. Trump will be second elected Presidency
Initiative"* — but adds the caveat the record would later lean on: *"Countermeasure being
implemented by Joe Biden... At this moment there is temporal agent from different parallel
dimension interfering."*

The outcome went otherwise, and the record's response became a doctrine in itself: the
[timeline-war framing](/lore/parallel-timelines/), in which predictions describe the
probability landscape of a moment, and *interference* — temporal agents, countermeasures,
[course corrections](/lore/election-2024/) — moves the outcome between tellings. The
archive's later [2024 Election Timeline](/lore/election-2024/) entry is the sequel: the
same machinery, revised by its own miss. Readers can weigh the caveat against the miss;
the record preserves both without adjustment.
